item.title: Gilts Surge on Lower Oil and Gas Prices After US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: UK government bonds jumped after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a sharp drop in energy prices.

item.title: Monte Paschi Fires CEO Lovaglio After Previously Ousting Him
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA formally removed ousted Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio after it had stripped him of his powers for defying the board’s plan to replace him.

item.title: Dubai Stocks Soar Most in a Decade on Iran War Ceasefire Relief
item.description: Dubai’s benchmark stock index surged the most in more than a decade after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in a conflict that had rattled Gulf markets and threatened energy infrastructure across the region.

item.title: RBI Governor Says Forex Market Curbs Won’t Remain Forever
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s recent currency market curbs aimed at quelling speculation against the rupee are temporary and will not remain in place forever, Governor Sanjay Malhotra said.

item.title: China’s LNG Demand Won’t Bounce Back From Middle East Turmoil
item.description: Hopes for a strong rebound in China’s demand for liquefied natural gas are fading, despite the ceasefire called in the Middle East, as analysts caution over lingering supply risks and higher prices.

item.title: Malaysia ‘Corporate Mafia’ Feud Takes New Turn as Mogul Sued
item.description: Malaysian businessman Victor Chin is suing labor tycoon Aminul Islam for the “attempted hijacking” of a company, the latest development in the so-called corporate mafia case that’s drawn public attention in the Southeast Asian nation.

item.title: German Bonds Jump as Energy Prices Slump on US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: German bonds and their euro area peers surged after energy prices sank following a ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: E&P of Ghana Wins Bid to Run Damang Mine After Gold Fields Exit
item.description: Engineers and Planners Co., a closely held company headed by the brother of Ghana’s president, won a bid to take over the Damang gold mine after Gold Fields Ltd.’s transfers the asset to the government.

item.title: Iran Ceasefire Fuels Hope of Cheaper Funding for Japanese Firms
item.description: A bond market rally triggered by the US-Iran ceasefire has fueled hopes for cheaper borrowing at least for one Japanese bond issuer.

item.title: Shell Says Oil Trade Profit Surged as Iran War Caused Chaos
item.description: Shell Plc said its first-quarter results were boosted by its oil trading operation, even as the company’s Middle East assets were battered by the Iran conflict.

item.title: Taiwan’s Foreign Reserves Drop Most Since 2011 on Intervention
item.description: Taiwan’s foreign reserves saw their steepest monthly drop in nearly 15 years in March, as the central bank sold the greenback to stabilize the local currency against capital outflows spurred by the Iran war.

item.title: German Factory Orders Showed Small Rebound Ahead of Iran War
item.description: German factory orders rebounded less than expected in February, before the start of the Iran war that risks derailing the recovery of Europe’s largest economy.

item.title: Malaysian Glove Maker WRP to Shut Down Over Iran War Disruptions
item.description: Malaysian rubber glove maker WRP Asia Pacific Sdn. will begin winding down its business operations this month, citing “severe disruptions across global energy and petrochemical supply chains” caused by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: European Gas Futures Tumble on US-Iran Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: European natural gas plunged after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that could temporarily reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz and ease strained global energy markets.

item.title: Revolut to Open Paris Office as Lender Seeks to Expand in Europe
item.description: Revolut Ltd. will soon open its Western European headquarters in Paris, as the UK fintech firm last valued at $75 billion looks to fuel its growth by expanding in the continent.

item.title: Stocks Set to Surge as Two-Week Ceasefire Agreed
Dollar Slides as Ceasefire Dents War’s Most High-Profile Haven
item.description: The dollar fell against all its major peers after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, sapping demand for the currency as a haven.

item.title: European Stocks Set for Biggest Gain Since 2022 on US-Iran Deal
item.description: European shares were set for their biggest advance since 2022 after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude plunged 15%.

item.title: Svatantra Microfin Said to Prepare for $250 Million India IPO
item.description: Advent International LP-backed Svatantra Microfin Pvt. has met with bankers and lawyers as the first step in the process for an initial public offering that could raise as much as $250 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Zhipu Hikes Prices Again as China AI Monetization Wave Quickens
item.description: Zhipu raised the cost of access to its most advanced AI model by at least 8%, joining other leading Chinese artificial intelligence players in trying to profit off years of research and computing investments.

item.title: Traders Line Up Wagers on Hungary’s Markets as Election Nears
item.description: With just days to go before Hungarians head to the polls, investors and strategists say there’s more room for markets to rally if polls are correct in showing that Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year-old experiment in “illiberal democracy” is coming to an end.

item.title: A Civilization Doesn’t Die, and Markets Go Up
item.description: Crude has fallen more only during Covid and the outset of Desert Storm.

item.title: Stock Futures Jump on US, Iran Agreement for Two-Week Ceasefire
item.description: US stock index futures rallied after President Donald Trump’s announcement of a two-week ceasefire in the Iran war triggered relief across markets.

item.title: Vietnam Names New Central Bank Head as War Threatens Growth Goal
item.description: Vietnam named the ex-head of the nation’s biggest agricultural lender to run the central bank, which is tasked with turbocharging growth despite tariffs, inflationary pressures and the war in the Middle East.

item.title: India Keeps Key Rate Steady as Weak Rupee Takes Center Stage
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India held interest rates in its first policy decision since the Middle East crisis erupted, as it grapples with a sharply weaker rupee while trying to support economic growth.

item.title: AI K-Pop Startup Galaxy Aims For IPO in Seoul, New York
item.description: Humanoid robots dressed in black, luxury hip-hop outfits snap into formation, moving in perfect sync to K-pop songs. It’s a simulation inside an office styled as a crash-landed spacecraft in the heart of Seoul’s financial district, complete with towering astronauts and floating robotic jellyfish.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank Seeks to Calm FX Fears as Taka Hits Record Low
item.description: Bangladesh’s central bank sought to reassure investors that the nation’s foreign-exchange market remains stable and the taka faces no immediate risk of depreciation, even as the currency slid to a fresh low against the dollar.

item.title: Japan’s Chiyoda Considers Resuming Work on Qatar LNG Project
item.description: Japan’s Chiyoda Corp. says it is considering resuming construction work on a giant liquefied natural gas export plant in Qatar, in a sign of cautious optimism hours after the US and Iran agreed a ceasefire.

item.title: Chinese Battery Maker Announces 11B Yuan Capacity Expansion Plan
item.description: EVE Energy Co., one of China’s top lithium-ion battery makers, said it would build two new manufacturing plants with a combined annual capacity of 110 gigawatt-hours at a total cost of 11 billion yuan ($1.6 billion).

item.title: Indonesia’s FX Reserves Hit Two-Year Low on Rupiah Defense
item.description: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a third straight month in March to the lowest level in nearly two years as the central bank stepped up market intervention to stabilize the falling rupiah.
